transliteration: adhyātma-jñāna-nityatvaṃ tattva-jñānārtha-darśanam | etaj jñānam iti proktam ajñānaṃ yad ato ’nyathā ||
literal_meaning: constancy in self-knowledge, philosophical vision of truth—this is declared knowledge; whatever is other than this is ignorance
contextual_meaning: Krishna caps the long list of virtues by naming the core of psychological maturity: sustained self-inquiry and unbroken devotion. Anything that distracts from this inner alignment is ignorance, not knowledge.
psychological_state: Post-insight consolidation—after the mind has catalogued every virtue, only steady self-awareness and wholehearted surrender remain as the non-negotiables.
trigger_scenario: A high-achieving client finishes listing all their self-improvement goals, then realizes none matter unless they keep a daily practice of honest self-reflection and genuine connection to something bigger than ego.
anchor_text: Stay rooted in self-knowledge; everything else is distraction.
